title: System Behavior
description: Configure the Windchill Home page, manage calendars and task delegation, and set preferences and properties to control system behavior across contexts.

topics.title: Configuring the Windchill Home Page

content.text: The Windchill Home page is the first screen users see after signing in, and it shapes their daily interaction with the system. Administrators can configure a standard Home page that applies to all users on the site, ensuring a consistent starting experience that highlights the most relevant information.
Public and Private Tabs

When signed in as an administrator, the Home page displays two tabs. The Private tab controls the administrator's own personal Home page settings. The Public tab enables the administrator to configure a standard Home page that is deployed to all users on the site. Changes made to the Public tab are carried forward to the Home pages of all users, except those who have already modified their own Home pages.

rows: Only the administrator who made the changes
Standard Home page for the entire site
All users who have not already customized their own Home page

content.text: Customizing the Home Page Layout

The default Home page includes tables for Tasks, Updates, and Checked Out Work. Using the Customize option in the upper-right corner of the Public tab, administrators can add or remove tables, rearrange the display order by dragging table hyperlinks, and configure which information is most prominent. Users always retain the ability to override the initially configured Home page to suit their own work preferences.

Configure the Public Home page early in your deployment. Users who customize their own Home pages before you set the Public tab will not receive your changes. Setting the standard layout first ensures all users start with a consistent, well-organized view.

content.prompt: Match each Home page concept to its description:

pairs.right: Controls the administrator's personal Home page settings
Configures a standard Home page deployed to all site users
Adds, removes, or rearranges tables on the Home page
Allows individual users to modify their own Home page layout

keyTakeaways: Administrators configure a standard Home page for all users via the Public tab
Changes to the Public tab only affect users who have not already customized their own Home pages
The Customize option allows adding, removing, and reordering tables on the Home page
Users always retain the ability to override the standard Home page to suit their own preferences

topics.title: Calendar Management

content.text: The Windchill calendar defines working and non-working days for the system, and it directly affects how deadlines are calculated. A workflow task with a 7-day deadline means 7 working days, not 7 calendar days. Proper calendar management ensures deadlines are accurate and prevents tasks from being trapped with unavailable users.
Calendar Scope and Types

Calendar maintenance is conducted at the Site context level, meaning there is a single system calendar that applies to the entire Windchill installation. Out of the box, Saturday and Sunday are defined as non-working days. Administrators can manage three different calendar scopes:

rows: Set the administrator's own working and non-working days
Manage working days and delegation for individual users
Define non-working days (holidays, company closures) that affect all deadline calculations

content.text: Task Delegation

When users are unavailable, Windchill supports task delegation. Users can delegate their work assignments to another user for a specified period of time or indefinitely. Delegation is configured on individual user calendars, not the system calendar. This ensures that workflows continue to progress even when participants are on vacation or leave.

Delegation is only available on individual user calendars. You cannot configure delegation at the system calendar level. If your organization spans multiple countries, remember that national holidays vary. Administrators should update the system calendar to reflect company-wide non-working days and rely on user calendars for individual absences.

content.prompt: Arrange these calendar management tasks from system-wide scope to individual scope:
items: Define company-wide non-working days on the System Calendar
Set national holiday exceptions on the System Calendar
Configure a user's vacation days on their User Calendar
Set up task delegation to a colleague on the User Calendar

keyTakeaways: Windchill deadlines are calculated in working days, not calendar days, based on the system calendar
Three calendar scopes exist: My Calendar, User Calendar, and System Calendar
Task delegation allows users to redirect work assignments to a colleague for a specified period or indefinitely
Delegation is configured on individual user calendars, not the system calendar

topics.title: Preferences and Properties

content.text: Windchill provides two distinct mechanisms for controlling system behavior: preferences and properties. Although both influence how the system operates, they differ fundamentally in scope, management method, and whether they require a server restart. Understanding this distinction is critical for business administrators.
Preferences

Preferences define default application behavior and can be set at the Site, Organization, or application context levels. They follow the context hierarchy, meaning child contexts inherit preference values from their parent. Administrators can lock preferences to prevent overrides in child contexts. In the Preference Manager, the term scope is synonymous with context. Users can also set personal preferences through My Settings, which override context-level values for that individual.

rows: Thumbnail images for 2D/3D content, table column defaults
Check-in defaults, save-as defaults, default object types
Latest version search behavior, search scope defaults
Revision label picker display count, allow override on revise
Display workspace preferences, workspace management options

content.text: Properties

Properties control low-level behavior and system functions. They are stored in configuration files in the Windchill installation directories (such as wt.properties) and are managed using the xconfmanager command-line tool. Unlike preferences, properties affect the entire system and require a Windchill server restart to take effect.

Never confuse preferences with properties. Preferences are managed through the UI, apply per-context, and take effect immediately (with a browser refresh at most). Properties are managed via xconfmanager, apply system-wide, and require a full server restart. Changing a property on a production server requires coordination with the system administrator and a planned maintenance window.

content.prompt: Match each characteristic to preferences or properties:

pairs.left: Can be set per-context and inherited

keyTakeaways: Preferences define default application behavior and are managed through the Preference Management UI without requiring a server restart
Properties control system-level behavior, are stored in configuration files, and require a server restart via xconfmanager
Preferences can be set at Site, Organization, or application context levels and follow the context inheritance hierarchy
Administrators can lock preferences to prevent child contexts from overriding them

topics.title: Exercise 6 - Configure the Windchill Home Page

content.text: In this exercise, you will standardize the Windchill Home page for all users at the site by modifying the Public tab display and then verifying that the changes propagate to a regular user account.
You need access to the Windchill training server. For Task 1, sign in as wcadmin/wcadmin (site administrator). For Task 2, sign in as a regular user (Arden Reed) to verify the changes.

content.title: Configure the Windchill Home Page
content.objective: Modify the default Home page display via the Public tab and verify that changes are visible to a regular user.

tasks.title: Modify the Default Home Page Display

steps.action: Launch a web browser and click the Windchill bookmark.
steps.detail: Open the Windchill sign-in page using the bookmark configured for your training environment.

steps.action: Sign in to Windchill: Username wcadmin, Password wcadmin.
steps.detail: The site administrator account has access to the Public tab on the Home page, which controls the standard layout for all users.

steps.action: Click the Home icon to display the Home page.
steps.detail: The Home page is the first screen users see after signing in. It contains tables showing tasks, updates, and other information.

steps.action: Select the Public tab and click the Customize menu to view all available tables.
steps.detail: The Public tab shows the current standard Home page layout that is deployed to all users who have not customized their own Home pages. The Customize menu lets you add or remove tables.
steps.hint: The Public tab is only visible to administrators. If you do not see it, verify you are signed in as wcadmin.

steps.action: Select the Workspaces and Discussions checkboxes.
steps.detail: Adding Workspaces gives users quick access to their active workspaces. Adding Discussions surfaces collaboration threads on the Home page.
steps.hint: If these tables are already present, choose other available tables to add.

steps.action: Change the table order: at the top of the Public tab, drag the Tasks hyperlink all the way to the left.
steps.detail: Table order determines the visual priority on the Home page. Placing Tasks first ensures users immediately see their pending work items.
steps.hint: Note: You cannot drag the tables themselves, only the hyperlinks at the top of the tab.

steps.action: Drag Workspaces to the end of the hyperlinks list.
steps.detail: Moving Workspaces to the last position keeps it accessible but gives priority to Tasks, Updates, and other tables.

steps.action: Close the web browser.
steps.detail: The Home page changes are saved automatically and will be deployed to all users who have not already customized their own Home page.

tasks.title: Verify the Home Page Changes for a User

steps.action: Launch a web browser and click the Windchill bookmark.
steps.detail: Open a new browser session to sign in as a different user and verify the Home page changes.

steps.action: On the Profiles page, select Arden Reed.
steps.detail: Signing in as a non-administrator account lets you verify that the Public tab changes have propagated to a standard user's Home page.
steps.hint: Use the credentials provided by your instructor for the Arden Reed account.

steps.action: Click the Home icon and notice that the default table display includes the Discussions and Workspaces tables.
steps.detail: If the user had not previously customized their Home page, your Public tab changes should be reflected. The Tasks table should appear first in the display order.
steps.hint: If the tables are not visible, the user may have previously customized their Home page, which overrides the Public tab settings.

steps.action: Close the web browser.
steps.detail: You have successfully configured a standard Home page layout and verified it appears for end users.

keyTakeaways: The Public tab on the Home page controls the standard layout for all site users
Workspaces and Discussions tables can be added to provide users with quick access to collaboration and workspace data
Users who have already customized their Home page are not affected by Public tab changes

topics.title: Exercise 7 - Delegate Tasks in Calendar

content.text: In this exercise, you will configure task delegation for a user who is taking two weeks off. Juan Edwards needs to delegate his work assignments to Kathleen Perez so that workflows continue to progress during his absence.
Sign in as jedwards/ptc for this exercise. You will configure delegation on Juan Edwards' personal calendar to redirect tasks to Kathleen Perez.

content.title: Delegate Tasks in Calendar
content.objective: Configure task delegation from Juan Edwards to Kathleen Perez for a two-week period using the User Calendar.

steps.action: Launch a web browser and select the Windchill bookmark.
steps.detail: Open the Windchill sign-in page using the bookmark configured for your training environment.

steps.action: Sign in to Windchill: Username jedwards, Password ptc.
steps.detail: Juan Edwards is the user who needs to delegate tasks. Delegation is configured from the user's own account via the Calendar settings.

steps.action: Select Quick Links > My Settings > Calendar.
steps.detail: The Calendar settings page shows the user's working and non-working days and provides the delegation configuration options.
steps.hint: Quick Links is typically accessible from the top navigation or the user menu.

steps.action: In the Calendar dialog, select the next Monday, press Shift, and select Friday two weeks later.
steps.detail: Selecting the specific date range defines the delegation period. Only working days need to be selected since non-working days (weekends) are already excluded from task deadlines.
steps.hint: If the weeks span a month, press Shift to select the days in the current month and then press Ctrl to select the remaining days.

steps.action: Select Delegate > Delegate Selected Days.
steps.detail: The Delegate action assigns all workflow tasks for the selected days to another user. This ensures that pending work items are routed to the delegate instead of remaining with the unavailable user.

steps.action: In the Find Participants dialog, search for the User Name kperez.
steps.detail: Search for the user who will receive the delegated tasks. Kathleen Perez is Juan's colleague who will handle his workflow tasks during the absence.
steps.hint: Type 'kperez' in the user name search field and click Search.

steps.action: In the Search Results, select the radio button for Kathleen Perez and click OK.
steps.detail: Selecting the user and clicking OK confirms the delegation. Kathleen Perez will receive Juan's workflow tasks for the selected period.

steps.action: Notice that Kathleen Perez now has delegated tasks from Juan Edwards for the selected days.
steps.detail: The calendar should display delegation indicators on the selected dates. During this period, any workflow tasks assigned to Juan Edwards will be automatically routed to Kathleen Perez.

steps.action: Close the calendar.
steps.detail: The delegation settings are saved and will take effect on the selected dates.

steps.action: Close the web browser.
steps.detail: You have successfully configured task delegation from Juan Edwards to Kathleen Perez for a two-week period.

keyTakeaways: Task delegation is configured from the user's own account via Quick Links > My Settings > Calendar
Delegation can be set for a specific date range using Shift-click and Ctrl-click to select working days
Delegated tasks are automatically routed to the specified colleague during the delegation period

topics.title: Exercise 8 - Set Preferences

content.text: In this exercise, you will configure revision-related preferences to ensure that technical editors have the correct revision choices when importing and revising documents. You will set preferences at both the Organization and Site levels and lock them to prevent overrides.
For Tasks 1 and 2, sign in as wcadmin/wcadmin to configure preferences. For Task 3, sign in as bpatterson/ptc to test the preference changes from a regular user's perspective.

content.title: Set Preferences
content.objective: Configure revision override and picker list preferences at the Organization and Site levels, lock them to prevent overrides, and verify the changes for a regular user.

tasks.title: Set the Revision Override Preference for an Organization

steps.action: Launch a web browser and click the Windchill bookmark.
steps.detail: Open the Windchill sign-in page using the bookmark configured for your training environment.

steps.action: Sign in to Windchill: Username wcadmin, Password wcadmin.
steps.detail: The site administrator has access to the Preference Management utility across all contexts.

steps.action: Expand the Navigator and select Organizations > PTC Publications.
steps.detail: Navigate to the PTC Publications organization to set preferences at the organization level. This scopes the preference change to this organization and its child contexts.
steps.hint: If PTC Publications is not visible, navigate to Organizations first and then open the PTC Publications context.

steps.action: Click Utilities.
steps.detail: The Utilities section provides access to administrative tools for the current context.

steps.action: In the Business Administration section, click Preference Management.
steps.detail: Preference Management is the administration tool for viewing and setting preferences at any context level.

steps.action: In the Preference Management table, expand the Revise node.
steps.detail: The Revise node contains preferences related to revision behavior, including override settings and display options.
steps.hint: Look for the Revise category in the preference tree on the left side of the Preference Management page.

steps.action: Right-click the Allow Override on Revise preference and select the Set Preference action.
steps.detail: The Set Preference action opens a dialog where you can change the preference value and optionally lock it.

steps.action: In the Set Preference dialog, set the value to Yes, select the Locked checkbox to apply this preference to all the application contexts belonging to the organization, then click OK.
steps.detail: Setting the value to Yes allows technical editors to override the default revision label when revising documents. Locking the preference prevents any child context (products, libraries) from changing this value, ensuring consistent revision behavior across the entire PTC Publications organization.

tasks.title: Set the Revision Picker List Size

steps.action: From the Navigator, select the Site context.
steps.detail: Switching to the Site context allows you to set a preference that applies across the entire Windchill installation, not just one organization.
steps.hint: Use the context breadcrumb or Browse menu to navigate to the Site level.

steps.action: Click Utilities.
steps.detail: Access the Utilities section to open Preference Management at the Site level.

steps.action: In the Business Administration section, click Preference Management.
steps.detail: Opening Preference Management from the Site context means any preferences you set here will apply system-wide.

steps.action: In the Preference Management table, expand the Revise node.
steps.detail: Navigate to the same Revise category to find the revision picker display count preference.

steps.action: Right-click the Revision Label Picker Display Count preference and select the Set Preference action.
steps.detail: This preference controls how many revision options appear in the revision picker dropdown when users revise a document.

steps.action: In the Value field, delete the existing value and type 15.
steps.detail: Increasing the count from the default value ensures that editors see enough revision choices to select the appropriate label. A value of 15 provides a wider range of revision options.

steps.action: Select the Locked checkbox to apply this preference to all contexts below the Site level. Click OK.
steps.detail: Locking at the Site level ensures no organization or application context can change this value. All users across the system will see up to 15 revision options in the picker.

steps.action: Close the web browser.
steps.detail: End the site administrator session before signing in as a different user to test the preferences.

steps.action: Launch a web browser and click the Windchill bookmark.
steps.detail: Open a new browser session to sign in as a regular user.

steps.action: Sign in to Windchill: Username bpatterson, Password ptc.
steps.detail: Signing in as a regular user lets you verify that the preference changes are visible and effective from an end-user perspective.

steps.action: From the Navigator, browse to Recent Products > PTC New Publication Template.
steps.detail: Navigate to an existing product that belongs to the PTC Publications organization where the revision override preference was set.

steps.action: Select the Folders link.
steps.detail: Navigate to the Folders view to access documents stored in the product context.

steps.action: Open the Team Information folder.
steps.detail: This folder contains documents that can be used to test the revision behavior.
steps.hint: If the folder path differs in your environment, navigate to any document that supports revision.

steps.action: Right-click SPECIFICATION 2 and select Revise.
steps.detail: The Revise action opens the revision dialog, which should now reflect the preferences you configured — allowing override and showing up to 15 revision options.

steps.action: In the New Revision column, click the Select Revision glyph.
steps.detail: The revision glyph opens the picker dropdown showing available revision labels.

steps.action: Expand the revision list and notice that you can select up to 15 revision options.
steps.detail: The revision picker should show more options than the default. This confirms that both the Site-level display count preference and the Organization-level override preference are active.
steps.hint: If fewer than 15 options appear, the total available revisions may be less than 15, which is expected. The key is that the limit has been raised.

steps.action: Click Cancel.
steps.detail: Cancel the revision dialog without making changes to the document.

steps.action: Close the web browser.
steps.detail: You have verified that locked preferences propagate correctly and affect end-user behavior without requiring a server restart.

keyTakeaways: Preferences can be set at different context levels (Site, Organization, application context) and inherited by child contexts
The Locked checkbox prevents child contexts from overriding a preference value
Preference changes take effect without a server restart, unlike properties which require a restart
Testing preferences from a regular user account verifies that the configuration is working as intended
